english: dignity are grown old and gone, when the position in life has been lost Glr.; hence go-rgás (go rgas) may be applied to an old maid (Schr.); raṅ-gi (rang gi) go (go) ºdug ('dug) that is my place, my business, like ča (cha); also place, space, spot in a still more general sense: 'a-mai ('a ma'i) gó-na (go na) at the place of my mother, with my mother Glr.; raṅ-tág-gi (rang thag gi) gó-na (go na) near the mill Glr.; go (go) ldóg-pa (ldog pa) (zlog-pa (zlog pa), lóg-pa (log pa)) to change place, esp. to turn to the contrary S.g.; nád-go (nad go) the seat of a disease Sch.; go-byéd (go byed) is mentioned as a quality of the air S.g.; spriṅ-gyi (sprin gyi) go-bar (go bar) ṗyé-nas (phye nas) oṅs ('ongs), we came parting the space between the clouds Mil.; spriṅ (sprin) dkar (dkar) ldiṅ-gi (lding gi) go-čòg (go cog) Mil.? — 3. armour, gen. gó-čá (go cha). — Comp. go-skábs (go skabs) lit. a chance of taking place, of existing, bdé-bai (bde ba'i) go-skábs (go skabs) gá-la (ga la) yod (yod) Pth., where is there a possibility of being happy? — go-skál (go skal) C. the share or portion due to a person in accordance to his rank. — go-kāṅ (go khang) arsenal Schr. — go-kráb (go khrab) coat of mail with helmet, armour. — go-grál (go gral) rank, dignity Cs. — go-grás (go gras) id. Cs. — go-rgás (go rgas) v. go (go) 2. — gó-ča (go cha) 1. armour; often fig.: bzód-pai (bzod pa'i) gó-ča (go cha) bgó-ba (bgo ba), or gón-pa (gon pa), to put on the armour of patience; mi-jigs-go-ča (mi 'jigs go cha) the harness of intrepidity. 2. gear, implements, tools in general, bkra-śis (bkra shis) srúṅ-bai (srung ba'i) gó-ča (go cha) (charmed) instruments used for securing future happiness (in behalf of a new-born infant) Med. — go-mnyám (go mnyam) C. of equal rank. — go-tém (go them) degree of dignity or rank Cs. — go-ºdún (go 'dun) = sna-ºdún (sna 'dun), of different sorts, various Lex. — go-ldóg (go ldog) (cf. go-ldóg-pa (go ldog pa)) the contrary, reverse, opposite; wrong, perverse, dé-las (de las) go-ldóg (go ldog) the contrary of it Med.: *go ('go) jug ('jug) go-lóg-la (go log la)* W. head fore-most; *go-lóg (go log) čó-če (co ce)* W. to go to work in the wrong way, *go-lóg (go log) ḍí-če (bri ce)* to write wrong. — go-pán(s) (go 'phang(s)) 1. degree, rank, dignity, blón-poi (blon po'i) go-pán-la (go 'phang la) bkód-par (bkod par) gyúr-ba (gyur ba) Pth. to be installed into the dignity of a minister; go-pán (go 'phang) spár-ba (spar ba) Lex. to raise the dignity. 2. model, pattern, standard of perfection (?) Cs. — go-mtsáms (go mtshams) v. go (go) no. 1. — go-mtsón (go mtshon) harness and weapons. — go-rím (go rim) 1. order of rank Glr. — 2. succession, successive order, turn.
the place (near the hearth) for firewood Mil.

I. vb. 1. to understand, comprehend, W. *há-go-če* (ha go ce); go-dká-ba (go dka' ba) difficult, hard to be understood, go-slá-ba (go sla ba) easy to be understood, intelligible; *gho-dé-wa yoṅ* (go bde ba yong) C. now it becomes intelligible, thus it will be understood; go-byed-brdá (go byed brda) Lex. an explaining, illustrating symbol; gó-žiṅ (go zhing) rtóg-pa (rtogs pa) to take in and comprehend; brdá-ru (brda ru) go (go) this I understand to be a symbol Pth.; gó-bai (go ba'i) yul (yul), gó-byai (go bya'i) yul (yul) a subject intelligible to all Schr. — 2. to mean, to imagine, par that. Glr.; go-nór-ba (go nor ba) to misunderstand, to mistake, to be mistaken. — II. sbst. perception, comprehension, gó-ba (go ba) bláṅ-ba (blang ba) Mil. to come to a right comprehension, a clear perception (of some philosophical or religious truth); gó-bai (go ba'i) mjál-ba (mjal ba) Lex.: 'mjál-ba (mjal ba) in the sense of perceiving'.
a large eagle or vulture, C., W. and B.; go-sér (go ser) the common black-bearded vulture of the Himalaya, with a yellow neck; go-brún (go brun) excrements of it Med.
Med. n. of a poisonous medicinal fruit Cs.; go-bye (go bye) Med. id.?
Med., n. of a flower Cs.
Cs.; 'prison, jail'; prop. a court surrounded by a wall.
v. go-ldóg (go ldog) sub go (go) Comp.
rank, dignity, *go-sá (go sa) čén-po (chen po)*, *tón-po (mthon po)* W. high in rank.

wylie: go ta ma, gau ta ma, gau tA ma
english: the Gotamide, the descendant of Gotama, which, among others, was the name of the founder of the Nyaya philo-
